primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange exposure from intragroup dividends (once the receivable is recorded) can be designated as a hedged item for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Defines that only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions involving a party external to the entity can be designated as hedged items, and explicitly prohibits hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements

Establishes the same external party requirement for hedged items in consolidated statements, with a specific exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items between entities with different functional currencies

supporting_authority.reason: Defines the scope of monetary items qualifying for foreign currency hedge accounting exception and establishes when exchange differences on intragroup items affect consolidated profit or loss
Clarifies that hedge accounting applies only to foreign exchange differences between functional currencies and confirms external party requirement for hedged items in net investment hedges

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosure requirements for hedge accounting but do not define eligibility criteria for hedged items
Classification requirements for gains/losses on hedging instruments but not relevant to determining hedged item eligibility

treatment_families.family: Intragroup items ineligible as hedged items in consolidated statements
mapped_approaches: No hedge accounting for intragroup dividends

treatment_families.family: Exception for intragroup monetary items with foreign currency risk

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intragroup dividends
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The primary authority (IAS 39/IFRS 9) prohibits designation of intragroup transactions as hedged items in consolidated financial statements; dividend receivables do not qualify for the foreign currency exception which applies only to monetary items with different functional currencies affecting consolidated profit or loss
